title: The Camel and the Arab
lines: A Camel and his master, an Arab, were traveling together through the desert.
When night came and a cold wind began to blow, the Camel asked his master for shelter.
'I am so cold,' said the Camel. 'May I put my head inside the tent?'
The Arab agreed, and the Camel put his head in.
Soon the Camel asked to put his neck in as well.
Gradually, the Camel pushed more and more of his body inside until the Arab was squeezed out.
'I gave you an inch and you took a mile," complained the Arab.
moral: Do not give an inch, lest those who are greedy take a mile.
